image.prompt: A pencil-sketch architectural blueprint showing a building's skeletal framework where certain load-bearing walls have been removed but the upper floors remain suspended in mid-air, supported by nothing—graphite lines depicting structural stress cracks spreading through the foundation. In the negative space, faint geometric forms suggest invisible scaffolding that was never installed. A single orange accent marks the point where weight concentrates with no support beneath. White background, matte finish, editorial minimalism evoking the visual language of The Economist's structural metaphors for organizational failure.
